chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 161 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on CMA No.777/2023 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 3. For orders on CMA No.778/2023 (Ex./A) 4. For orders on CMA No.779/2023 (Stay) 5. For hearing of main case 02.02.2023 Mr. Jeewan Khan Kalhoro, Advocate for the petitioners..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- 1. Urgency is granted. 2 to 5. Petitioner No.1 Mst. Shar Naz is present and states that on 05.01.2023, being sui juris, she has contracted marriage with petitioner No.2, namely, Ghulam Shabbir without any compulsion and coercion by swearing affidavit (copies of affidavit and nikahnama are available as Annexure ‘B’ and ‘B/1’ at Page 21 and 23, respectively). She further states that since she has contracted love marriage with petitioner No.2, her family members are very much annoyed and are issuing threats of dire consequences. Besides, her uncle has lodged a false F.I.R. bearing No.01 of 2023 at Police Station Taju Dero, District Jacobabad under Section 365-B, P.P.C. She further states that she has not been abducted or enticed by anyone including petitioner No.2; therefore, she has approached this Court for legal protection. At the very outset, learned Counsel for the petitioners states that so far the quashment of aforementioned F.I.R. is concerned, the petitioners shall approach to Circuit Bench of this Court at Larkana; however, he seeks directions to respondent No.6 (S.H.O., Police Station Bhiria, District C. P. No. D – 161 of 2023 Page 2 of 2 Naushahro Feroze) to provide legal protection to the petitioners as there is apprehension that the family members of petitioner No.1 may cause harm to the lives of the petitioners.

No. D – 161 of 2023 Page 2 of 2 Naushahro Feroze) to provide legal protection to the petitioners as there is apprehension that the family members of petitioner No.1 may cause harm to the lives of the petitioners. The instant petition is disposed of by directing to respondent No.6 to provide legal protection to the petitioners, as and when they approach to him. The petitioners, however, are at liberty to approach the concerned Court for quashment of the aforesaid F.I.R. Office is directed to communicate this order to respondent No.6 for compliance. The petition stands disposed of in the above terms along with listed applications. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
